extraction.one_line_summary: VINGEGAARD attacked early on the 4.8km/9% final climb, distanced KUSS by 29 seconds and ROGLIC to move into second overall.
tactical_patterns: VINGEGAARD attacked on steepest gradients in second half of 4.8km climb, reversing expected ROGLIC sprint finish pattern.
Jumbo-Visma sent TRATNIK alone to bridge breakaway, demonstrating team strength demoralizes rivals rather than conserving domestiques.
GROVES drove breakaway pace solo for 10 miles pre-sprint despite being pure sprinter, sacrificing recovery for points classification.
EVENEPOEL sat up and lost time deliberately, suggesting stage hunting incompatible with Jumbo-Visma's break-chasing control.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: attacked aggressively early on final climb despite claiming stomach issues first week, claims third-week improvement coming
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: negative: lost 29 seconds to teammate after leading race, vulnerability exposed on steep second-half gradients
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: expected to sprint finish but dropped when VINGEGAARD attacked early, tactical surprise neutralized his strength
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: deliberately abandoned GC defense by sitting up, repositioning for stage hunting without Jumbo chase pressure
rider_signals.POELS Wout: positive: finished strong in chase group, showing climbing form despite domestique role
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: positive: rode powerfully on front for extended period despite sprinter profile, unusual endurance display
rider_signals.FISHER-BLACK Finn: positive: 21-year-old held solo effort to finish 40 seconds down on VINGEGAARD, surprising climbing strength
